scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every path in DocuSeal's published OpenAPI spec (21 operations across /templates, /submissions, /submitters) is enumerated below, fetched directly from console.docuseal.com/openapi.yml. Read streams cover every practical GET (list + a new `template_detail` single-object stream); writes.json now covers every dialect-expressible mutation with a plain JSON body and no binary/multipart payload (create_submission, archive_submission, update_submitter, update_template, archive_template, clone_template). Endpoints excluded below are genuinely binary-payload (PDF/DOCX file upload) or destructive/composite-file operations outside the JSON-only declarative write dialect.

excluded.reason: single-submission detail retrieval (nested submitters/documents/events) not modeled as its own stream; the `submissions` list stream already surfaces every scalar field this bundle's schema projects, and a per-id detail fan-out is not implemented this pass
single-submitter detail retrieval not modeled as its own stream; the `submitters` list stream already covers every field this bundle's schema projects
returns signed document file references/binary content, not tabular record data
creates a submission by parsing signature requests from inbound emails; a niche ingestion path with no legacy precedent, not a general-purpose reverse-ETL write

requires a base64/URL-embedded PDF document payload; the declarative write dialect has no multipart/binary body support (conventions.md §1 Tier-2 trigger list — multipart/binary bodies are a hook-only capability, and no hook package is being added in this pass)
requires a base64/URL-embedded DOCX document payload; same binary-body limitation as /submissions/pdf
creates a submission directly from raw HTML document markup; a document-authoring path, not a reverse-ETL data write on an existing template/submission resource
requires a base64/URL-embedded PDF document payload to create a new template; same binary-body limitation as /submissions/pdf
requires a base64/URL-embedded DOCX document payload to create a new template; same binary-body limitation
creates a template directly from raw HTML document markup; a document-authoring path, not a reverse-ETL data write
merges multiple existing templates' documents into one new template; a document-composition operation with no legacy precedent and no natural single-record write shape
replaces a template's underlying document files; requires a base64/URL file payload, same binary-body limitation
